mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and residential investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields a negative or zero 60-day forward return in over 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where INDPRO increases, specifically during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPI exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll growth and unemployment decline signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue potential, systematically generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with GDP growth rates (p-value > 0.10) across multiple expansion and contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when industrial production drops below the -0.5% threshold.
